food_name: Campbell's Condensed Cream of Mushroom Soup (prepared with equal water)

english: Campbell's condensed cream of mushroom soup

romanized: Jīn Bǎo Nǎi Yóu Mó Gū Tāng

cuisine: Commercial canned food / Singapore home cooking
region_of_origin: United States (Campbell Soup Company); widely used in Singapore households
food_subgroup: Condensed soup — requires dilution with equal volume of water before consumption; 1 serving = 1/2 can (approx. 153g condensed) prepared with 153ml water yielding ~245–250ml soup

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn covers cream-based soups generically under canned soup category; the specific condensed-form Campbell's product is not individually addressed. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Campbell's Condensed Cream of Mushroom Soup. A0FKP (canned condensed soup) used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for this commercial condensed soup product. Not assigned to avoid fabrication.

preparation_method: diluted with equal volume of water before serving

serving.description: 1/2 can condensed (153g) prepared with 153ml water = ~245ml soup
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is half a 305g can of condensed soup diluted with an equal volume of water, yielding approximately 245ml of prepared soup.

nutrient_source.title: Campbell's Condensed Cream of Mushroom Soup — Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (305g can)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore-market product NIP on the 305g can. Values represent the prepared soup (condensed diluted 1:1 with water). Per-100g values are as reported on the label for the prepared product. Cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entry for cream of mushroom soup, canned, condensed, prepared with equal volume water (FDC ID 173590) for secondary validation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of cream-based soups with wheat flour thickener — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for cream soups and wheat-thickened products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Campbell's Condensed Cream of Mushroom Soup in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~65 (medium) is an analogous estimate based on: (1) cream-based soups with wheat flour thickener typically fall in the GI 60–70 range in the Sydney database; (2) the wheat flour and modified starch thickener contribute rapidly-digestible starch. GL per 245ml serving = (65 × 4.9g carbohydrate per 100g × 2.45) / 100 ≈ 7.8g, classified as moderate GL. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content — 347mg per 100g prepared soup; 850mg per standard 245ml serving (42.5% of 2,000mg/day limit in a single bowl)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should be specifically counselled that Campbell's Condensed Cream of Mushroom Soup is a major hidden sodium source: one prepared serving delivers 850mg sodium, and when used undiluted as a cooking ingredient in Singapore rice cooker meals, a single portion may contribute 425–850mg sodium before any additional salt or soy sauce is added — clinicians must ask about this product explicitly in dietary recalls.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~GL 8 per serving) from wheat flour and modified starch thickener; sodium is the more pressing clinical concern
type2_diabetes.guidance: The GL of approximately 8 per 245ml serving is moderate and unlikely to cause significant postprandial glucose excursion when consumed as a soup; however, when used undiluted as a cooking sauce in rice-based meals, the carbohydrate contribution is more concentrated and should be accounted for in meal planning alongside the high sodium load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (850mg/serving) and saturated fat (0.6g/100g; ~1.5g/serving) with cholesterol 5mg/100g; ultra-processed product classification
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For patients with cardiovascular disease, this product's very high sodium content (850mg per prepared serving) poses the primary risk for blood pressure elevation and fluid retention; saturated fat is modest but the product's ultra-processed nature and sodium density make it unsuitable for regular consumption in a heart-healthy diet, and patients should be advised to limit use to occasional servings with no additional salt.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very high sodium exacerbates fluid retention in RA patients on NSAIDs; dairy cream contributes saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Not recommended as a regular dietary component for RA patients; use only occasionally with full awareness of sodium load, and avoid combining with other high-sodium condiments.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Campbell's Condensed Cream of Mushroom Soup is an ultra-processed canned soup product manufactured by the Campbell Soup Company and sold in Singapore in a standard 305g can. The condensed product must be diluted with an equal volume of water before consumption, yielding approximately 610ml of prepared soup from one can. One standard serving is half a can (approximately 153g condensed) prepared with 153ml water, yielding ~245ml of soup. The soup has a smooth, creamy texture with a mild mushroom flavour derived from pureed mushrooms, wheat flour thickener, cream, and salt. Nutritionally, the prepared soup is relatively low in energy (41 kcal/100g) and macronutrients, but is extremely high in sodium — 347mg per 100g prepared, or approximately 850mg per serving, representing 42.5% of the recommended daily sodium limit in a single bowl. Clinically, this product is most significant in Singapore not as a standalone soup but as the most common hidden sodium cooking ingredient in Singapore households. The iconic red-and-white tin is routinely used undiluted as a sauce base for mushroom chicken rice cooker meals, baked pasta, casseroles, and one-pot family meals. When used in this way, one can distributed across 2–4 portions still delivers 425–850mg sodium per person per meal from this single ingredient alone, before any additional soy sauce, salt, or seasoning. Patients with hypertension who report not adding salt to their food may still be consuming very high sodium via Campbell's and similar condensed cooking products. The glycemic load per serving is moderate (~GL 8), driven by wheat flour and modified starch thickeners. Allergens include wheat (gluten) and dairy (cream). The product is vegetarian but not vegan or halal-certified.

how much sodium in Campbell's mushroom soup

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore-market product Nutrition Information Panel (305g can) by Campbell Soup Company, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 173590) for cream of mushroom soup prepared with equal volume water. All core macros and secondary nutrients are confirmed from the product label. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework for cream-based wheat-thickened soups.
notes: vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg are reported as 0.0 — confirmed zero per the Singapore product NIP; these vitamins are not present in meaningful quantities in this product. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per product label. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1.0: detailed fatty acid breakdown is not reported on the Singapore product NIP and is not available in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this specific product. water_g is set to -1.0: moisture content is not reported on the product NIP. The halal status of this product in Singapore is not confirmed on the product label reviewed; consumers requiring halal-certified products should verify with the manufacturer. Two portions are listed: 250g (1 bowl, general reference) and 245ml (1/2 can prepared, verified serving). GI is an analogous estimate (confidence 0.9) — no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the access date.
